summary: Large mpLib decompilation/matching PR centered on src/melee/mp/mplib.c, adding many previously stubbed mpLib functions, tightening mpLib data structures and callback signatures, and propagating the corrected types into collision, stage, item, fighter, camera, and symbol metadata. The decomp.dev bot reported +5648 matched bytes, 31 new matches, and 7 improved unmatched functions, with most wins in main/melee/mp/mplib.
what_changed: Implemented or improved a large block of mpLib functions in src/melee/mp/mplib.c. Bot-reported new full matches included mpLib_80051EC8, mpLib_80055C5C, mpLib_8004EBF8, mpLib_80054DFC, mpLib_8005389C, mpLib_80053950, mpLib_80058560, mpLib_80054B14, mpLib_80054BC0, mpLib_800565DC, mpLib_80054D68, mpLib_80058ACC, mpLib_80057528, mpLib_800575B0, mpLib_8005811C, mpLib_80054C6C, mpLib_80054CEC, mpLib_80055E24, mpLib_80057B4C, mpLib_80056758, mpLib_80057FDC, mpLib_80058044, mpLib_8005667C, mpLib_80056710, mpLib_800524DC, mpLib_80052508, mpLib_800566D8, mpLib_800580FC, mpLib_800580AC, and mpLib_800580E0.
Improved additional mpLib functions without fully matching them, including bot-reported progress on mpLib_8005A340, mpLib_80051BA8, mpLib_80059404, mpLib_80056B6C, and mpLib_8004E97C.
Reworked mpLib callback typing in src/melee/mp/forward.h: mpLib_Callback now takes Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32; the separate mpLib_GroundCallback typedef was removed.

Propagated the corrected callback signature into stage code, especially Ice Mountain and Kongo: fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, fn_801F9558, grIceMt_801FA7F0, fn_801D7700, and fn_801D7E60 gained typed Ground*, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and float parameters instead of UNK_T-heavy prototypes.

Refined mpLib data structures in src/melee/mp/types.h and src/melee/mp/mplib.static.h, including mpLib_804D64C0_t fields such as next, nested x4 entry data, bitfield xE, float bounds/coordinates x10-x1C, mpLib_Callback x24, and Ground* x28.

Adjusted mpColl local variables and callback storage from integer placeholders to Ground* and mpLib_Callback, using NULL rather than 0 for pointer initialization and removing integer casts around pointer outputs to mpLib_800524DC.

Adjusted item collision helpers it_8026EA20 and it_8026EA9C from misleading Vec3*/s32 forms toward UNK_T*/int* pointer arguments, and updated Link Arrow call sites with scratch int locals and explicit UNK_T casts for item variable addresses.

Made small matching-oriented stack/local type changes across fighter and item code, such as replacing f32 stack temporaries with int in ftCo_800A9904, ftCo_800AAF48, ftCo_800AB224, ftCo_800B732C, ftCo_800B7638, and itlinkarrow collision helpers.
Updated camera linkage by exposing cm_804D6468 as extern in camera.h and changing the static header declaration from static CameraBox* to CameraBox*.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt data boundaries for mpLib data and split several newly identified data strings at lbl_803BF480, lbl_803BF490, lbl_803BF4A0, lbl_803BF4B0, lbl_803BF4CC, lbl_803BF4F4, and lbl_803BF570.
smart_moves: The PR did not treat mplib.c in isolation: it matched mpLib by correcting the types consumed by mpColl, stages, items, and headers, which reduced cast noise and made callback calls compile into the expected ABI shape.
Replacing the old loose mpLib_GroundCallback/UNK_T prototypes with one explicit mpLib_Callback signature aligned callback storage, registration, lookup, and invocation across mpLib and mpColl.
The struct work in mp/types.h converted opaque fields into enough concrete layout to unlock many functions while preserving STATIC_ASSERT size checks on known structures.
Pointer-vs-integer cleanup was used tactically for matching: Ground* locals initialized with NULL, int* outputs to mpLib_800524DC, and CameraBox* linkage all fixed codegen-sensitive type information.
The symbol map was updated to split mpLib data objects and strings instead of leaving oversized aggregate symbols, improving future data matching and searchability.
decomp_lessons: For mpLib/collision work, callback signatures are decomp-critical. A single wrong typedef can force incorrect local types and casts through stage and collision call chains.

When a library owns callbacks into stage code, fix both sides together: registration/lookup functions such as mpLib_800580FC/mpLib_800581BC and caller code in mpColl must agree with stage callback prototypes.
Use concrete pointer types instead of s32 placeholders once evidence supports them; here Ground*, CollData*, int*, and mpLib_Callback improved both readability and matching.
Stack local type choices can matter even when storage size is identical. Several f32 locals were changed to int to improve codegen in fighter/item callers.
Data symbol boundaries matter for matching. Splitting strings and correcting object sizes in symbols.txt can accompany C matches when large .data aggregates were masking real objects.
Keep STATIC_ASSERTs around refined structs so future layout changes remain constrained while fields are gradually named.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based names were retained for unresolved mpLib functions, e.g. mpLib_80051EC8, mpLib_80055C5C, mpLib_800524DC, and mpLib_8005A340.
Callback typedef naming was consolidated to mpLib_Callback; the more specific but apparently inaccurate mpLib_GroundCallback typedef was removed.
Enum values kept the mpLib_GroundEnum_UnkN style, e.g. mpLib_GroundEnum_Unk2, reflecting still-unknown semantic names.
Partially understood mpLib structs kept address/global-derived names such as mpLib_804D64B8_t and mpLib_804D64C0_t, with field names like x10/x14/x18/x1C where semantics are still unknown.
New data-string labels in symbols.txt used lbl_803BFxxx names where no semantic names were available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed mpLib_8004E97C parameters from double to float in the static prototype, a classic PPC codegen-sensitive correction for avoiding unwanted double-precision calling/conversion behavior.
Used int stack temporaries instead of f32/Vec3* placeholders where the assembly expected integer/pointer scratch storage, notably in fighter functions and itlinkarrow collision code.
Used NULL for pointer initialization and removed casts such as (int) sp14 when passing int* outputs to mpLib_800524DC, preserving pointer-typed call ABI while keeping identical storage.
Introduced nested struct fields and bitfield xE in mpLib_804D64C0_t to express accessed offsets directly rather than relying on opaque pads.
Kept or used alternative-code scaffolding around mpColl_80043558 with #if SOLUTION == 0 while refining the local declarations to Ground* and mpLib_Callback.
Adjusted call wrappers like mpLib_800524DC and mpLib_80052508 to forward to mpLib_80051EC8 with fixed flag masks, matching small wrapper functions.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
The only recorded comment was the decomp.dev bot report: GALE01 matched code rose to 44.07%, +0.15%, +5648 bytes, with 31 new matches and 7 improvements.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename mpLib_80051EC8 or mpLib_800524DC after this matching pass.
Search for subsequent uses or refinements of mpLib_Callback and mpLib_GroundEnum in stage callback code.
Investigate later struct naming for mpLib_804D64C0_t and its nested x4 collision/line data.
Look for future PRs that remove the remaining #if SOLUTION block around mpColl_80043558.
Search whether mpLib_8005A340, mpLib_80051BA8, mpLib_80059404, and mpLib_80056B6C later reached 100%.
